methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Hyfix is a Seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $15M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of four same-sector, same- or adjacent-stage peers (Stark Defence at $500M Series A, Vatn Systems at $300M Series A, Ares Industries at $150M Series A, and Tenna Systems at $60M Seed), alongside public market reference points.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $15M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a typical Seed-to-next-stage multiplier of 2.5x, implies a valuation of $38M. Peer-median analysis across the four comparables yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. This peer set skews toward Series A valuations, which inflates the median relative to Hyfix's current stage.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $32M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $131M. The wide dispersion reflects material stage discount between Hyfix and the predominantly Series A peer set. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue traction and capital efficiency relative to peers, as well as the applicability of later-stage comparables to a Seed-stage business. Narrowing the range would require additional Seed-stage defense-sector data or near-term progress metrics on product-market fit.
